The text does not provide specific information about near-term catalysts that could improve results.

The text does not provide specific information about environmental regulations and compliance costs.

The competitive environment is intense, with competitors resorting to price competition and private-label brands.

evidence.text: The markets in which we operate are very competitive... Some of our competitors may resort to price competition to sustain or grow market share and manufacturing capacity utilization. Also, certain large customers continue to offer private-label brands that compete with some of our product offerings as a lower-cost alternative.
evidence.subsection: We operate in very competitive consumer and trade brand categories.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments due to the impact of inventory reductions by distribution channel partners.

evidence.text: Net sales decreased by $78.1 million, or 1.6%, due to slowing housing market activity in China, lower sales unit volume in our Water segment due to the impact of inventory reductions by our distribution channel partners, lower sales demand in the U.S. and Canada and higher sales incentives, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $41 million. These factors were partially offset by price increases to help mitigate the impact of cumulative commodity and transportation cost increases in all our segments and the benefit from the Solar and Aqualisa acquisitions ($50.2 million combined).

factors.summary: The Water segment is experiencing slower demand, while the Outdoors and Security segments are performing better.

evidence.text: Net sales decreased by $191.0 million, or 6.9%, due primarily to slowing housing market activity in China, lower sales unit volume driven by inventory reductions by our distribution channel partners, lower sales demand in the U.S. and Canada, and higher promotion and sales rebate costs, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $30 million. These factors were partially offset by the benefit from price increases to help mitigate the impact of cumulative commodity and transportation cost increases, the benefit from the Aqualisa acquisition ($22 million) and sales increases in our U.S. e-commerce channel.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, putting pressure on margins.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses decreased by $15.8 million, or 1.4%, due to lower net sales and lower advertising, marketing and headcount related costs. These factors were partially offset by higher transportation costs and the impact of acquisitions.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed, with reductions in certain segments.

evidence.text: Net sales decreased by $78.1 million, or 1.6%, due to slowing housing market activity in China, lower sales unit volume in our Water segment due to the impact of inventory reductions by our distribution channel partners, lower sales demand in the U.S. and Canada and higher sales incentives, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $41 million. These factors were partially offset by price increases to help mitigate the impact of cumulative commodity and transportation cost increases in all our segments and the benefit from the Solar and Aqualisa acquisitions ($50.2 million combined).

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is ongoing, with severance and restructuring charges in 2022.

evidence.text: Restructuring charges of $32.4 million in 2022 are largely related to severance, asset impairment and other costs associated with plant closures and headcount actions primarily in the Water and Outdoors segments. Restructuring charges of $9.3 million in 2021 were largely related to severance costs associated with the relocation of manufacturing facilities within our Outdoors and Security segments.

factors.summary: Digital transformation initiatives are ongoing, with higher consulting costs in 2023.

evidence.text: Corporate expenses increased by $19.4 million, or 17.3%, due to higher consulting costs relating to our digital transformation initiatives.

factors.summary: New products are being launched, with the Solar and Aqualisa acquisitions contributing to revenue.

evidence.text: In June 2023, we acquired the ASSA Businesses from ASSA. [...] In July 2022, we acquired 100% of the outstanding equity of Aqualisa for a purchase price of $156.0 million, net of cash acquired of $4.8 million. [...] In January 2022, we acquired 100% of the outstanding equity of Solar for a purchase price of $61.6 million, net of cash acquired of $4.8 million.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating across all regions, driven by weaker housing market activity, lower sales volumes, and unfavorable foreign exchange. The company is facing challenges in both the U.S. and international markets, with additional pressure from higher costs.

evidence.text: During 2023, the U.S. home products market contracted due to decreases in repair and remodel and new home construction activity.
The pace of single-family and existing home sales activity and new home construction and repair and remodel activities has slowed, which adversely impacted our results.

Net sales decreased by $78.1 million, or 1.6%, due to slowing housing market activity in China, lower sales unit volume in our Water segment due to the impact of inventory reductions by our distribution channel partners, lower sales demand in the U.S. and Canada and higher sales incentives, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $41 million.

factors.summary: Demand trends are negative, with decelerating overall demand particularly noted in both the U.S. market and the Water segment. Factors contributing to this include slowing housing market activity, inventory reductions by distribution partners, lower sales demand in the U.S. and Canada, higher sales incentives, and unfavorable foreign exchange.

evidence.text: The U.S. market for our products primarily consists of spending on both new home construction and repair and remodel activities within existing homes, with a substantial majority of the markets we serve consisting of repair and remodel spending. Continued growth in the U.S. market for our home products will largely depend on consumer confidence, employment, wage growth, home prices, stable mortgage rates and credit availability. Increases in inflation and mortgage rates during the preceding years have slowed the pace of single-family and existing home sales activity and new home construction and repair and remodel activities.

Net sales decreased by $78.1 million, or 1.6%, due to slowing housing market activity in China, lower sales unit volume in our Water segment due to the impact of inventory reductions by our distribution channel partners, lower sales demand in the U.S. and Canada and higher sales incentives, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $41 million.

factors.summary: Volume trends are negative, with unit volumes declining across multiple segments, including Total Fortune Brands and Water, driven by factors such as lower sales activity, economic conditions, and supply chain issues. The declines are particularly pronounced in the Water segment, which saw a significant decrease in net sales.

evidence.text: Net sales decreased by $96.8 million, or 2.0%, due to lower sales unit volume in the U.S. and lower sales in our international markets ($41.2 million) as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $24 million.
The pace of single-family and existing home sales activity and new home construction and repair and remodel activities has slowed, which adversely impacted our results.

Net sales decreased by $191.0 million, or 6.9%, due primarily to slowing housing market activity in China, lower sales unit volume driven by inventory reductions by our distribution channel partners, lower sales demand in the U.S. and Canada, and higher promotion and sales rebate costs, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $30 million. These factors were partially offset by the benefit from price increases to help mitigate the impact of cumulative commodity and transportation cost increases, the benefit from the Aqualisa acquisition ($22 million) and sales increases in our U.S. e-commerce channel.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing significant challenges with rising input costs, primarily driven by inflation, supply chain disruptions, and energy price fluctuations. These cost increases are impacting various aspects of their operations, including raw materials, labor, and freight. While the company is attempting to offset these unfavorable impacts through price increases and productivity improvements, the overall situation remains challenging with ongoing pressures on margins.

evidence.text: We have been and may continue to be impacted by near-term supply, labor and freight constraints, a volatile global supply chain environment, as well as sustained increased rates of inflation, increased interest rates, unfavorable fluctuations in foreign exchange rates and the ongoing costs of tariffs.
The company is facing challenges with rising labor costs, including wage inflation and benefit expenses, which are putting pressure on margins.

These factors were partially offset by price increases to help mitigate the impact of cumulative commodity and transportation cost increases in all our segments and the benefit from the Solar and Aqualisa acquisitions ($50.2 million combined).

While there is evidence of ongoing efforts, such as plant closures and headcount reductions, the full benefits of these initiatives have not yet been realized, and cost pressures may not be fully offset.

factors.summary: The company's productivity improvements have partially offset certain cost pressures, such as higher transportation and restructuring costs. However, operational efficiency could face challenges due to changes in assumptions and economic conditions, particularly concerning inventory levels and workforce management.

evidence.text: Productivity improvements are helping to offset some cost pressures.
evidence.subsection: Recently Issued Accounting Standards / Critical Accounting Estimates

evidence.text: Operating income decreased by $36.8 million, or 4.5%, primarily due to lower net sales, higher commodity costs, separation and other transaction costs related to the Separation ($83 million in 2022, of which $73 million is presented within discontinued operations), and higher transportation, restructuring, sales rebate and headcount related costs, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $12 million. These factors were partially offset by the benefit from productivity improvements, favorable geographic channel mix in the Water segment and lower advertising and marketing costs.

The assumptions used to determine the estimated fair value of reporting units for impairment testing are forecasted revenue growth rates, operating income margins, market-participant discount rates and EBITDA multiples.

factors.summary: The company has been highly active in merger and acquisition activities, particularly with significant purchases in 2023, including the ASSA Businesses, Aqualisa, and Solar. These acquisitions are strategic and are expected to create value for the company.

evidence.text: In June 2023, we acquired the Emtek and Schaub premium and luxury door and cabinet hardware business (the 'Emtek and Schaub Business') and the U.S. and Canadian Yale and August residential smart locks business (the 'Yale and August Business', and, collectively with the Emtek and Schaub Business, the 'ASSA Businesses') from ASSA ABLOY, Inc. and its affiliates ('ASSA'). The Company completed the acquisition for a total purchase price of approximately $809.3 million, subject to post-closing adjustments, net of cash acquired of $16.3 million.

In June 2023, we acquired the ASSA Businesses from ASSA. [...] In July 2022, we acquired 100% of the outstanding equity of Aqualisa for a purchase price of $156.0 million, net of cash acquired of $4.8 million. [...] In January 2022, we acquired 100% of the outstanding equity of Solar for a purchase price of $61.6 million, net of cash acquired of $4.8 million.

factors.summary: Management has not explicitly adjusted forward guidance, but the emphasis on annual operating plans and cost reduction initiatives indicates a cautiously optimistic stance.

evidence.text: Our projection for the U.S. new home starts and home repair remodel spending, our annual operating plans finalized in the fourth quarter of each year, and our ability to execute on various planned cost reduction initiatives supporting operating income improvements.

factors.summary: Margins are under significant pressure across multiple areas, including higher costs, restructuring charges, foreign exchange fluctuations, and competitive dynamics. While there are mentions of partial offsets such as productivity improvements and favorable channel mix, the overall trend indicates compression with various headwinds impacting both gross and operating margins.

evidence.text: Operating income decreased by $159.4 million, or 20.6%, primarily due to lower sales unit volume in the U.S., lower international sales ($41.2 million decrease), manufacturing inefficiencies related to lower sales unit volume, amortization of the inventory fair-value adjustment related to the acquisition of the ASSA Businesses ($12.4 million), higher restructuring and other costs associated with the planned closure of a manufacturing facility in our Security segment, higher headcount-related costs, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $7.9 million.
Inventories... our inventory provision was $75.8 million and $49.2 million as of December 30, 2023 and December 31, 2022, respectively.

evidence.text: Our businesses may be adversely affected by poor performance or other disruptions in our distribution channels and customers... The strong competition that we face in all of our businesses may adversely affect our profitability and revenue levels, as well as our results of operations, cash flows and financial condition.

These factors were partially offset by the benefit from productivity improvements, favorable geographic channel mix in the Water segment and lower advertising and marketing costs.

factors.summary: Management's confidence appears mixed. While they exhibit confidence through ongoing share repurchases and dividend payments, they also show caution due to reduced revenue growth expectations and cost reduction initiatives.

evidence.text: During the fourth quarter of 2023, a reduction in revenue growth expectations, which were finalized during our annual planning process, led us to conclude that it was more likely than not that two indefinite-lived tradenames within our Outdoors segment were impaired.
evidence.subsection: Recently Issued Accounting Standards / Critical Accounting Estimates

evidence.text: In 2023, we repurchased 2.5 million shares of our outstanding common stock under the Company’s share repurchase program for $150.0 million.
We expect capital spending in 2024 to be in the range of $175 million to $225 million.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks such as margin compression from inventory provisions, labor costs, and uncertain economic conditions, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange and geopolitical factors. However, there is no specific information provided regarding risks from pension plans.

evidence.text: The fair values of the impaired tradenames were measured using the relief-from-royalty approach, which estimates the present value of royalty income that could be hypothetically earned by licensing the tradename to a third party over its remaining useful life. Some of the more significant assumptions inherent in estimating the fair values include forecasted revenue growth rates, assumed royalty rates, and market-participant discount rates that reflect the level of risk associated with the tradenames’ future revenues and profitability.
evidence.subsection: Recently Issued Accounting Standards / Critical Accounting Estimates

evidence.text: Foreign exchange movements are creating margin compression.

factors.summary: The company's key sensitivities include changes in discount rates, revenue growth rates, EBITDA multiples, commodity prices, interest rates, and foreign exchange movements, which can significantly impact the fair value of assets and future financial performance. While the Pension Plans subsection did not provide specific information on sensitivities, other areas highlight the importance of these factors in managing financial risks.

evidence.text: The significant assumptions that are used to determine the estimated fair value of reporting units for impairment testing are forecasted revenue growth rates, operating income margins, market-participant discount rates and EBITDA multiples.

factors.summary: The supply chain is facing ongoing disruptions, primarily due to labor shortages, transportation issues, and other constraints. While there is no specific mention of improvement, the operational and sourcing risks section highlights persistent challenges.

evidence.text: Supply chain disruptions could continue to impact our ability to timely source necessary components and inputs.

factors.summary: The company anticipates capital expenditures in the range of $175 million to $225 million for 2024, reflecting confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We expect capital spending in 2024 to be in the range of $175 million to $225 million.

factors.summary: The company is increasing its debt leverage, as evidenced by higher average borrowings and increased interest rates, which have led to a significant rise in interest expense.

evidence.text: Debt leverage is increasing, with higher interest rates and borrowings.
Interest expense increased by $34.9 million, or 41.4%, due to higher average interest rates and higher average borrowings.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is centered on enhancing market presence through resource allocation to key segments and indefinite-lived intangible assets, alongside investments in digital transformation and strategic transactions to boost shareholder value. While the recently issued accounting standards section highlights significant allocations to segments like Water, Outdoors, and Security, the corporate section emphasizes digital initiatives and financial flexibility for future investments.

evidence.text: Of our $520.1 million indefinite-lived tradenames, $216.9 million relate to our Water segment, $271.2 million relate to our Outdoors segment and $32.0 million relate to our Security segment as of December 30, 2023.
evidence.subsection: Recently Issued Accounting Standards / Critical Accounting Estimates

evidence.text: Our principal sources of liquidity are cash on hand, cash flows from operating activities, cash borrowed under our credit facility and cash from debt issuances in the capital markets. [...] We believe our operating cash flows, including funds available under the credit facility and access to capital markets, provide sufficient liquidity to support the Company’s working capital requirements, capital expenditures, other contractual commitments and service of indebtedness, as well as to finance acquisitions, repurchase shares of our common stock and pay dividends to stockholders, as the Board of Directors deems appropriate, both for the 12-month period following the 2023 fiscal year, and in the long-term.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns have been addressed through consistent dividend payments and share repurchases. Specifically, the company paid $116.8 million in dividends and repurchased $150 million worth of shares in 2023. However, the Pension Plans subsection did not provide specific information on shareholder returns, indicating a potential gap in disclosure regarding this aspect. Overall, the evidence suggests a positive approach to returning value to shareholders through these mechanisms, despite the lack of detail in one section.

evidence.text: In 2023, we repurchased 2.5 million shares of our outstanding common stock under the Company’s share repurchase program for $150.0 million. [...] In 2023, we paid dividends in the amount of $116.8 million to the Company’s stockholders.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, driven by strong operating cash flows.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $1,055.8 million in 2023, compared to $566.3 million in 2022. The $489.5 million increase in net cash provided by operating activities from 2022 to 2023 was driven by a $702.5 million increase in net cash provided by continuing operations due to an increase in net cash provided by working capital items resulting from initiatives to align working capital with current U.S. home product market activity and expected sales volume.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rate changes on the company is predominantly negative. Increased interest rates have led to higher borrowing costs, negatively affecting the company's financial performance. Additionally, rising interest rates, combined with inflation and supply chain issues, have slowed sales and construction activity, further impacting the company's results.

evidence.text: Interest expense increased by $34.9 million, or 41.4%, due to higher average interest rates and higher average borrowings.

Due to heightened inflation and increases in interest rates, combined with labor and supply chain constraints, during 2022 and 2023, the pace of single-family and existing home sales activity and new home construction and repair and remodel activities has slowed, which adversely impacted our results, and, although interest rates have decreased in recent months, it is uncertain when such activities will recover.

factors.summary: The economic environment presents significant challenges, primarily due to heightened inflation, increased interest rates, and supply chain constraints. These factors have negatively impacted various sectors, including housing markets and foreign exchange rates, leading to decreased sales and market activity.

evidence.text: During 2022 and 2023, the pace of single-family and existing home sales activity and new home construction and repair and remodel activities has slowed, which adversely impacted our results, and, although interest rates have decreased in recent months, it is uncertain when such activities will recover.

Net sales decreased by $78.1 million, or 1.6%, due to slowing housing market activity in China, lower sales unit volume in our Water segment due to the impact of inventory reductions by our distribution channel partners, lower sales demand in the U.S. and Canada and higher sales incentives, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $41 million.

factors.summary: The company faces potential litigation exposure primarily due to cybersecurity risks, which could result in significant financial, legal, and operational consequences.

evidence.text: Failures in our information technology systems and the costs of increasing information security regulation could also subject us to significant financial, legal and operational consequences.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are negatively impacting margins.

evidence.text: Net sales decreased by $78.1 million, or 1.6%, due to slowing housing market activity in China, lower sales unit volume in our Water segment due to the impact of inventory reductions by our distribution channel partners, lower sales demand in the U.S. and Canada and higher sales incentives, as well as unfavorable foreign exchange of approximately $41 million. These factors were partially offset by price increases to help mitigate the impact of cumulative commodity and transportation cost increases in all our segments and the benefit from the Solar and Aqualisa acquisitions ($50.2 million combined).

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated the ability to pass on costs to customers through price increases, which has helped mitigate cost pressures. However, there is no guarantee this practice will continue in the future, as market conditions could change.

evidence.text: While in the past we have been able to mitigate the impact of these cost increases through productivity improvements and passing on increasing costs to our customers over time, there is no assurance that we will be able to offset such cost increases in the future.
